<title level="1" right="Animal Companion 1" pfs="Standard">[Badger](/AnimalCompanions.aspx?ID=1)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Animal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=531" />
<trait label="Minion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=653" />
</traits>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 215

Your companion is a badger, wolverine, or other big mustelid.

<column gap="tiny">

 

**Size** Small

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws,
**Damage** 1d8 piercing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claw ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)),
**Damage** 1d6 slashing

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +2

**Dex** +2

**Con** +2

**Int** -4

**Wis** +2

**Cha** +0
</row>

**Hit Points** 8

**Skill** [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16)

**Senses** [low-light vision](/Rules.aspx?ID=416), [scent](/Rules.aspx?ID=417) (imprecise, 30 feet)

**Speed** 25 feet, burrow 10 feet, climb 10 feet

 

**Support Benefit** Your badger digs around your foe’s position, interfering with its footing. Until the start of your next turn, if you hit and deal damage to a creature your badger threatens, the target can’t use a Step action (unless it can Step through difficult terrain) until it moves from its current position.

**Advanced Maneuver** Badger Rage

</column>

<document level="2" id="action-14" />
name: Badger

<traits>
<trait label="Animal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=531" />
<trait label="Minion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=653" />
</traits>

**Source** [Pathfinder #149: Against the Scarlet Triad](/Sources.aspx?ID=14) pg. 80

Your companion is a hyena

<column gap="tiny">

 

**Size** Small

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)),
**Damage** 1d8 piercing

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +2

**Dex** +3

**Con** +2

**Int** -4

**Wis** +1

**Cha** +0
</row>

**Hit Points** 6

**Skill** [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7)

**Senses** [low-light vision](/Rules.aspx?ID=416), [scent](/Rules.aspx?ID=417) (imprecise, 30 feet)

**Speed** 40 feet

 

**Support Benefit** The hyena mockingly yowls at your enemies and bites at them when you create an opening. Until the start of your next turn, each time you hit a creature in the hyena’s reach with a Strike, the creature becomes [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19).

**Advanced Maneuver** Gnaw

</column>

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Animal Companion 6" pfs="Standard">[Riding Tarantula](/AnimalCompanions.aspx?ID=100)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Animal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=531" />
<trait label="Minion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=653" />
</traits>

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 95

Your companion is a massive hairy spider.

<column gap="tiny">

 

**Size** Large

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fangs ([finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602)),
**Damage** 1d6 piercing plus poison

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
leg ([agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602)),
**Damage** 1d4 piercing

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +2

**Dex** +3

**Con** +1

**Int** -4

**Wis** +2

**Cha** +0
</row>

**Hit Points** 4

**Skill** [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48)

**Senses** [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59)

**Speed** 30 feet, climb 30 feet

**Special** [mount](/Rules.aspx?ID=3417) at 8th level; your riding tarantula’s fangs attack deals an additional 1d4 poison damage or 2d4 poison damage if the riding tarantula is a specialized companion

**Support Benefit** Your riding tarantula flicks urticating hairs at your foe, disrupting their focus. Until the start of your next turn, if you hit and deal damage to a creature your riding tarantula threatens, the target must succeed at a DC 5 flat check whenever it takes a [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561) action or that action is lost.

**Advanced Maneuver** Hair Barrage

</column>

<document level="2" id="action-2796" />
name: Riding Tarantula

<title level="1" right="Animal Companion 16" pfs="Standard">[Roc](/AnimalCompanions.aspx?ID=101)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Animal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=531" />
<trait label="Minion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=653" />
</traits>

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 95

Your companion is a younger example of the legendary bird of prey.

<column gap="tiny">

 

**Size** Huge

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
beak,
**Damage** 1d10 piercing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
talon ([agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526)),
**Damage** 1d8 slashing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45)

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +3

**Dex** +1

**Con** +3

**Int** -4

**Wis** +1

**Cha** +0
</row>

**Hit Points** 8

**Skill** [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40)

**Senses** [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=69)

**Speed** 15 feet, fly 60 feet

**Special** [mount](/Rules.aspx?ID=3417)

**Support Benefit** Your roc beats its wings, creating a powerful downdraft. Until the start of your next turn, your Strikes that damage a creature that your roc threatens [push](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) the target 5 feet away from your roc.

**Advanced Maneuver** Snatch

</column>

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Animal Companion 14" pfs="Standard">[Umbrella Mushroom](/AnimalCompanions.aspx?ID=102)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Fungus" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=614" />
<trait label="Minion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=653" />
</traits>

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 95

Your companion is a wide-capped mushroom capable of floating on air currents. A loop at the base of the stem acts as a stirrup for riding. An umbrella mushroom has the [fungus](/Traits.aspx?ID=614) trait instead of the [animal](/Traits.aspx?ID=531) trait, but it otherwise functions normally as an animal companion.

<column gap="tiny">

 

**Size** Large

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
stalk,
**Damage** 1d8 bludgeoning

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +2

**Dex** +2

**Con** +3

**Int** -4

**Wis** +1

**Cha** +0
</row>

**Hit Points** 6

**Skill** [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48)

**Senses** [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=69)

**Speed** 15 feet, fly 30 feet

**Special** [mount](/Rules.aspx?ID=3417); when you use [Command an Animal](/Actions.aspx?ID=2400) to command your umbrella mushroom, it gains a reaction it can only use for its Float advanced maneuver

**Support Benefit** Your umbrella mushroom exudes sense dulling spores when you create an opening. Until the start of your next turn, your Strikes that damage a creature within your umbrella mushroom's reach becomes [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 for 1 round. This effect has the [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) trait.

**Advanced Maneuver** Float

</column>

<document level="2" id="action-2798" />
name: Umbrella Mushroom

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Animal Companion 1" pfs="Standard">[Durian Crab](/AnimalCompanions.aspx?ID=108)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Animal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=531" />
<trait label="Minion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=653" />
</traits>

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 122

In the southern provinces of Nagajor, Xa Hoi, throughout the archipelago of Minata, and hidden within the thick overgrowth of the Valashmai Jungle are the notorious killer crustaceans known only as durian crabs. Using their large, powerful claws, they scale the trunks of tall trees, hiding their presence away from even the most observant of prey. When the time is right, they fall, plummeting onto their prey and letting the momentum and prickly thorns of their shells earn them their quarry.

<column gap="tiny">

 

**Size** Small or Medium

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claw ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526)),
**Damage** 1d6 piercing

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +3

**Dex** +2

**Con** +1

**Int** -4

**Wis** +2

**Cha** +0
</row>

**Hit Points** 6

**Skill** [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48)

**Senses** [Darkvision](/Familiars.aspx?ID=142)

**Speed** 20 feet, climb 20 feet, swim 20 feet

 

**Support Benefit** Your durian crab launches itself as part of your ranged attack to throw your enemies off balance. Until the start of your next turn, as long as your crab is within your reach, your ranged Strikes that deal damage to a creature within 30 feet make the target [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) until the end of your next turn. Your crab scuttles or launches itself back to the same space from which you threw it immediately after making a [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306), allowing you to hurl it multiple times.

**Advanced Maneuver** Crab Cannon

</column>

<document level="2" id="action-2974" />
name: Durian Crab

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Animal Companion 1" pfs="Standard">[Hermit Krait](/AnimalCompanions.aspx?ID=109)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Animal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=531" />
<trait label="Minion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=653" />
</traits>

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 122

There are countless tales of young children wallowing in the shallow waters, pressing the mouth of a seemingly empty shell to their ears, hoping to hear roaring waves, only to be met with a reptilian hiss and the bite of two fangs. There’s more than meets the eye when it comes to hermit kraits, in multiple ways. On the surface, the creature seems like a mundane shell taken over by a den of snakes, but any attempt to pry it from its home will only kill it, revealing a strange, reptilian creature with a multitude of heads.

<column gap="tiny">

 

**Size** Small

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fangs ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602)),
**Damage** 1d6 piercing

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +2

**Dex** +3

**Con** +2

**Int** -4

**Wis** +1

**Cha** +0
</row>

**Hit Points** 6

**Skill** [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48)

**Senses** [low-light vision](/Rules.aspx?ID=2411), [scent](/Rules.aspx?ID=2412) (imprecise, 30 feet)

**Speed** 20 feet, burrow 15 feet, swim 20 feet

 

**Support Benefit** Your hermit krait bites at your foes with venomous fangs. Until the start of your next turn, your Strikes that damage a creature in your hermit krait’s reach also deal 1d6 [persistent poison damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86). If your hermit krait is [nimble](/Companions.aspx?Type=Advancement&ID=7) or [savage](/Companions.aspx?Type=Advancement&ID=8), the persistent poison damage increases to 2d6.

**Advanced Maneuver** Defensive Curl

</column>

<document level="2" id="action-2975" />
name: Hermit Krait

<title level="1" right="Animal Companion 1" pfs="Limited">[Vulture](/AnimalCompanions.aspx?ID=11)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Animal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=531" />
<trait label="Minion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=653" />
</traits>

**Source** [Pathfinder #149: Against the Scarlet Triad](/Sources.aspx?ID=14) pg. 80

Your companion is a vulture, condor, or other large scavenging bird.

<column gap="tiny">

 

**Size** Small

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)),
**Damage** 1d8 piercing

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +2

**Dex** +2

**Con** +2

**Int** -4

**Wis** +2

**Cha** +0
</row>

**Hit Points** 6

**Skill** [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16)

**Senses** [low-light vision](/Rules.aspx?ID=416), [scent](/Rules.aspx?ID=417) (imprecise, 30 feet)

**Speed** 10 feet, fly 45 feet

**Special** Your vulture gains a +1 status bonus to all saves against disease, and if it rolls a success on a save against a disease, the result is a critical success instead.

**Support Benefit** The vulture retches and vomits onto a foe within 10 feet. Until the start of your next turn, if you hit and deal damage to the target, the target must succeed at a Fortitude save or become [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) (sickened 2 on a critical failure). The DC of the Fortitude save is 12 (or 14, if the vulture is a specialized animal companion) + your level + the vulture’s Constitution modifier.

**Advanced Maneuver** Feast on the Fallen

</column>

<document level="2" id="action-348" />

<title level="1" right="Animal Companion 1" pfs="Standard">[Sundaflora](/AnimalCompanions.aspx?ID=110)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Animal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=531" />
<trait label="Minion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=653" />
</traits>

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 122

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Characters from Tian Xia have access to the sundaflora animal companion._

Sundafloras are friendly, bipedal creatures with brightly colored, overlapping waves of petal-like scales covering most of their body. Though rare to find, they're most common in the artificial garden landscapes of Goka, usually seen waltzing around the grounds with their hands clasped together, giving off a pensive appearance.

<column gap="tiny">

 

**Size** Small

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
clubbed tail,
**Damage** 1d8 bludgeoning

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claws ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526)),
**Damage** 1d6 slashing

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +2

**Dex** +3

**Con** +1

**Int** -4

**Wis** +1

**Cha** +1
</row>

**Hit Points** 6

**Skill** [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45)

**Senses** [low-light vision](/Rules.aspx?ID=2411), [scent](/Rules.aspx?ID=2412) (imprecise, 30 feet)

**Speed** 20 feet, swim 15 feet

 

**Support Benefit** Your sundaflora inspires your allies with its calm demeanor and agile steps. Choose one ally within 30 feet of your sundaflora. They gain a +1 circumstance bonus to their next attack roll to Strike a foe within your sundaflora’s reach. The bonus lasts until the first time your ally uses it or until the beginning of your next turn, whichever comes first.

**Advanced Maneuver** Flower Dance

</column>

text: Sundaflora Source Tian Xia Character Guide pg. 122 PFS Note Characters from Tian Xia have access to the sundaflora animal companion. Sundafloras are friendly, bipedal creatures with brightly colored, overlapping waves of petal-like scales covering most of their body. Though rare to find, they're most common in the artificial garden landscapes of Goka, usually seen waltzing around the grounds with their hands clasped together, giving off a pensive appearance. <title level="1" right="Animal Companion 1" pfs="Standard">[Tikar Urchinpad](/AnimalCompanions.aspx?ID=111)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Animal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=531" />
<trait label="Minion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=653" />
</traits>

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 123

Tikar urchinpads are curious creatures that look similar to [jellyfish](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=140), have heads that resemble a floating plant or pad, and make use of their slimy tendrils to consume creatures that attempt to rest on the urchinpad’s head. Tikar urchinpads tend to float about in still water, soaking in the warmth of sunlight, not a care in the world. To the untrained eye, they appear only as a myriad of small, colorful squares sprawled out across a round pad, forming silhouettes of shapes that dazzle across the water’s surface.

<column gap="tiny">

 

**Size** Small or Medium

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
tendril ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602)),
**Damage** 1d6 slashing

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +2

**Dex** +3

**Con** +2

**Int** -4

**Wis** +1

**Cha** +0
</row>

**Hit Points** 6

**Skill** [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48)

**Senses** [scent](/Rules.aspx?ID=2412)(precise, 10 feet)

**Speed** 10 feet, swim 40 feet

 

**Support Benefit** Your tikar urchinpad attempts to wrap its tendrils around your foe’s limbs, pulling it off balance. Until the end of your next turn, as long as you’re adjacent to your urchinpad, if you attempt to [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=2380) or [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=2382) an opponent and roll a critical failure, you get a failure instead.

**Advanced Maneuver** Wrapping Tendrils

</column>

<document level="2" id="action-2977" />
name: Tikar Urchinpad

<title level="1" right="Animal Companion 1" pfs="Standard">[Yzobu](/AnimalCompanions.aspx?ID=112)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Animal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=531" />
<trait label="Minion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=653" />
</traits>

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 123

[Yzobus](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1374)are large equines abundant through the northeastern regions of Tian Xia, from the provinces of Hongal down to middle Tian Xia in the regions of Quain and Po Li. Here, herds of hundreds, if not thousands, have established themselves as nomads who have trekked from one end of the continent to the other as an unchanging tide of nature. Naturally, this resilience has led to their incorporation into these regions’ steeds and livestock, despite their inherent foul stench.

<column gap="tiny">

 

**Size** Large

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
horn,
**Damage** 1d8 piercing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
hoof ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526)),
**Damage** 1d6 bludgeoning

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +3

**Dex** +2

**Con** +2

**Int** -4

**Wis** +1

**Cha** +0
</row>

**Hit Points** 8

**Skill** [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49)

**Senses** [low-light vision](/Rules.aspx?ID=2411), [scent](/Rules.aspx?ID=2412) (imprecise, 30 feet)

**Speed** 40 feet

**Special** [mount](/Actions.aspx?ID=2314)

**Support Benefit** The next creature to make a melee attack against the yzobu or its rider before the start of your next turn is overwhelmed by the creature’s terrible stench and must attempt a Fortitude save. This DC is equal to your class DC or spell DC, whichever is higher. On a failure, the attacker takes a –2 circumstance penalty to its attack roll. This ability has the [olfactory](/Traits.aspx?ID=664) trait.

**Advanced Maneuver** Raging Stampede

</column>

<document level="2" id="action-2978" />
name: Yzobu

<title level="1" right="Animal Companion 1" pfs="Limited">[War Pig](/AnimalCompanions.aspx?ID=113)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Animal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=531" />
<trait label="Minion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=653" />
</traits>

**Source** [Pathfinder #208: Hoof, Cinder, and Storm](/Sources.aspx?ID=250) pg. 79

Belkzen’s native swine is the [bristle boar](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3754), a fearsome beast whose spines make it unsuitable for riding. Millennia ago, orcs seized domesticated pigs from neighboring lands and back-bred them to create the more fearsome [war pigs](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3766). These animals are large enough to carry an armored orc into combat, hardy enough to weather Belkzen’s badlands, and indiscriminate enough in their palate to subsist on whatever sustenance they can root up.

To the inexperienced handler, a war pig is a moody pet at best, though they’re quite intelligent and curious. Without proper enrichment and training, however, they seek their own entertainment—often by destroying gardens, eating spellbooks, or undermining the foundation of a small house. They’re also good at recognizing words, as war pigs have been known to destroy their pens if they hear the name of (but do not receive) their favorite treat.

<column gap="tiny">

 

**Size** Large

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
hoof ([agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526)),
**Damage** 1d6 bludgeoning

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
tusk,
**Damage** 1d8 piercing

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +3

**Dex** +1

**Con** +2

**Int** -4

**Wis** +1

**Cha** +1
</row>

**Hit Points** 8

**Skill** [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40)

**Senses** [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=69), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=2407)) 30 feet

**Speed** 35 feet

**Special** mount

**Support Benefit** Your war pig bodychecks your enemies, leaving them numb from the impact. Until the start of your next turn, [Strikes](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) you make that deal damage to a creature within your war pig’s reach make the target [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 1 until the end of your next turn.

**Advanced Maneuver** Bucking Frenzy

<document level="2" id="action-3168" />

</column>

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Animal Companion 10" pfs="Limited">[Wyvern](/AnimalCompanions.aspx?ID=114)</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Triumph of the Tusk</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Dragon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=581" />
<trait label="Minion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=653" />
</traits>

**Source** [Pathfinder #209: Destroyer's Doom](/Sources.aspx?ID=251) pg. 81

Though most wyverns are too prideful and headstrong to consider debasing themselves as “pets,” the [orcs](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=66) living near Wyvernsting have a reputation for training these [drakes](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=404) to serve as flying mounts. Exactly why wyverns acquiesce is unclear, though common theories include wyverns receiving treasure in exchange, being hatched in captivity so that they’ve never known freedom, or (most likely) that their orc trainers demonstrate such power and confidence that the drakes behave out of admiration. Over time, these wyverns’ most rebellious instincts have been quelled through selective breeding; otherwise the rate at which drakes eat their riders would be much, much higher. 

Even with these friendlier characteristics, few riders are tough enough for a wyvern companion. These creatures expect their handlers to be mentally and physically strong, earning a place among the wyvern’s artificial flock. Especially steady wyverns can be trained as mounts, though their innate pride makes this a rather dangerous proposition; if the drake begins to doubt a rider’s commands, they might simply ignore orders or even buck their rider mid-air.

This wyvern is an advanced companion , available only to characters of 10th level or higher. At the GM’s discretion, a wyvern companion might be accessible at lower levels, but if so, it doesn’t gain the Unsteady Mount ability until 10th level. Before then, it might function as a mount but be unable to fly with a rider, or its ability to fly with a rider might only be usable for 1 round per 10 minutes.

<column gap="tiny">

 

**Size** Large

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
stinger,
**Damage** 1d6 piercing plus poison

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws,
**Damage** 1d8 piercing

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +2

**Dex** +2

**Con** +2

**Int** -4

**Wis** +1

**Cha** +0
</row>

**Hit Points** 8

**Skill** [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48)

**Senses** [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=2407)) 30 feet

**Speed** 20 feet, fly 40 feet

**Special** The wyvern’s stinger attack deals additional poison damage equal to 1 + 1 per weapon damage die. 

**Unsteady Mount** The wyvern has the mount ability but is only strong enough to [fly](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312) in short bursts while carrying a rider; if it doesn’t end its turn on solid ground, it descends 30 feet without taking falling damage. At 14th level, a wyvern replaces this with the mount ability, removing this restriction.

**Support Benefit** Your wyvern whips its stinger at your foes. Until the start of your next turn, your [Strikes](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167) that damage a creature in your wyvern’s reach also deal 1d6 [persistent poison damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86). If your wyvern is [nimble](/Companions.aspx?Type=Advancement&ID=7) or [savage](/Companions.aspx?Type=Advancement&ID=8), the persistent damage increases to 2d6.

**Advanced Maneuver** Diving Skewer

<document level="2" id="action-3169" />

</column>
name: Wyvern

Until the start of your next turn, creatures within your bacallia’s reach that you damage with your Strikes are dazzled. Advanced Maneuver Spit

<title level="1" right="Animal Companion 1" pfs="Standard">[Keulia](/AnimalCompanions.aspx?ID=117)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Animal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=531" />
<trait label="Minion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=653" />
</traits>

**Source** [Shining Kingdoms](/Sources.aspx?ID=265) pg. 77

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Characters from Druma have access to this option._

Your companion is a horned frilled lizard with a unique debris “shell” that can consist of precious metals and gems.

<column gap="tiny">

 

**Size** Small

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws,
**Damage** 1d6 piercing

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +2

**Dex** +2

**Con** +3

**Int** -4

**Wis** +1

**Cha** +0
</row>

**Hit Points** 4

**Skill** [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50)

**Senses** [low-light vision](/Rules.aspx?ID=2411)

**Speed** 30 feet, burrow 10 feet

 

**Support Benefit** With its hard shell of objects, your keulia rams into enemies when you create an opening. Until the start of your next turn, each time you hit a creature in your keulia’s reach with a [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) and deal physical damage, the creatures takes 1d8 bludgeoning damage from the keulia. If your keulia is [nimble](/Companions.aspx?Type=Advancement&ID=7) or [savage](/Companions.aspx?Type=Advancement&ID=8), the bludgeoning damage increases to 2d8.

**Advanced Maneuver** Lizard Leap 

<document level="2" id="action-3240" />

</column>

<title level="1" right="Animal Companion 1" pfs="Standard">[Cragstone Bear](/AnimalCompanions.aspx?ID=118)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Animal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=531" />
<trait label="Minion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=653" />
</traits>

**Source** [Shining Kingdoms](/Sources.aspx?ID=265) pg. 99

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Characters from Five Kings Mountains have access to this option._

Your companion is a four-legged cave ursine whose diet includes mineral-rich cave water that causes plates of rock to grow over its fur. Playful and energetic, the cragstone bear tends to have a personality more like a loyal dog than a grizzly, but with its rocky hide, its romping can be more forceful than intended.

<column gap="tiny">

 

**Size** Small

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
slam,
**Damage** 1d8 bludgeoning

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claw ([agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526)),
**Damage** 1d6 slashing

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +3

**Dex** +1

**Con** +3

**Int** -4

**Wis** +1

**Cha** +0
</row>

**Hit Points** 8

**Skill** [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49)

**Senses** [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=82) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=2407), 20 feet)

**Speed** 35 feet

 

**Support Benefit** Your cragstone bear eagerly defends you. Until the start of your next turn, you gain a +1 circumstance bonus to your AC if your cragstone bear is adjacent to you. If your bear is [nimble](/Companions.aspx?Type=Advancement&ID=7) or [savage](/Companions.aspx?Type=Advancement&ID=8), increase the bonus to +2.

**Advanced Maneuver** Rolling Charge 

<document level="2" id="action-3241" />

</column>
name: Cragstone Bear

<title level="1" right="Animal Companion 1" pfs="Standard">[Giant Rock Tuatara](/AnimalCompanions.aspx?ID=119)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Animal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=531" />
<trait label="Minion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=653" />
<trait label="Minion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=653" />
</traits>

**Source** [Shining Kingdoms](/Sources.aspx?ID=265) pg. 100

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Characters from Five Kings Mountains have access to this option._

Your companion is a much larger cousin of the common rock tuatara, up to 6 feet long and 4 feet tall. Their milky-white third eye, spiny backs, and knobby club tails make them look far fiercer than they typically are.

<column gap="tiny">

 

**Size** Medium

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
tail slam,
**Damage** 1d8 bludgeoning

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
bite,
**Damage** 1d6 piercing

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +3

**Dex** +2

**Con** +2

**Int** -4

**Wis** +1

**Cha** +0
</row>

**Hit Points** 8

**Skill** [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40)

**Senses** [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=2407), 30 feet)

**Speed** 25 feet

 

**Support Benefit** Your giant rock tuatara trips up your enemies when you create an opening. Until the start of your next turn, your [Strikes](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) that deal damage to a creature within your giant rock tuatara’s reach make the target [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 1 until the end of your next turn.

**Advanced Maneuver** Tail Escape 

<document level="2" id="action-3242" />

</column>
name: Giant Rock Tuatara

<title level="1" right="Animal Companion 1" pfs="Limited">[Cave Gecko](/AnimalCompanions.aspx?ID=12)</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Extinction Curse</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Animal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=531" />
<trait label="Minion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=653" />
</traits>

**Source** [Pathfinder #154: Siege of the Dinosaurs](/Sources.aspx?ID=31) pg. 73

Your companion is a sticky-footed lizard like a gecko or anole.

<column gap="tiny">

 

**Size** Small

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)),
**Damage** 1d6 piercing

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +2

**Dex** +3

**Con** +1

**Int** -4

**Wis** +2

**Cha** +0
</row>

**Hit Points** 6

**Skill** [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15)

**Senses** [low-light vision](/Rules.aspx?ID=416), [scent](/Rules.aspx?ID=417) (imprecise) 30 feet

**Speed** 25 feet, climb 25 feet

 

**Support Benefit** Your cave gecko helps you stay afoot, leaning into you and propping you up as you maneuver your opponent. Until the end of your next turn, as long as you are adjacent to your cave gecko, if you attempt to [Disarm](/Actions.aspx?ID=41), [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=38), or [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=40) an opponent and roll a critical failure, you get a failure instead.

**Advanced Maneuver** Tongue Pull

</column>

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Animal Companion 1" pfs="Standard">[Gray Bladeling](/AnimalCompanions.aspx?ID=120)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Construct" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=35" />
<trait label="Minion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=653" />
<trait label="Undead" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=160" />
</traits>

**Source** [Shining Kingdoms](/Sources.aspx?ID=265) pg. 123

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Characters from Galt have access to this option._

Your companion is a vaguely humanoid construct composed of razor-sharp blades and animated by an amalgamation of restless soul fragments, similar to a miniature [gray death](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1641). Although it possesses no identity or memories from the souls it contains, it’s driven by a powerful but unfocused hate that its master must sometimes struggle to control.

<column gap="tiny">

**Access** You're from Galt. 

**Size** Small

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
blade ([agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602)),
**Damage** 1d8 slashing

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +2

**Dex** +1

**Con** +2

**Int** -4

**Wis** +0

**Cha** +1
</row>

**Hit Points** 10

**Skill** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36)

**Senses** [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [lifesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=67) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=2407)) 30 feet

**Speed** 20 feet

**Special** A gray bladeling has the [construct](/Traits.aspx?ID=563) and [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722) traits instead of the [animal](/Traits.aspx?ID=531) trait. It has [immunity](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) to [bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), [death effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=2331), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68), [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [nonlethal attacks](/Rules.aspx?ID=2311), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), and [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91). Unlike most undead, it becomes [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95) and [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=69) rather than being destroyed instantly at 0 Hit Points; unlike most constructs, its Hit Points can be restored by only [void healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=83) instead of any other actions that restore Hit Points to objects or nonliving creatures.

**Support Benefit** The gray bladeling slashes viciously at your enemy when you create an opening. Until the start of your next turn, each time you hit a creature in the bladeling’s reach with a [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306), the creature takes 1d4 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) or becomes [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1 if it’s already taking persistent bleed damage. If the gray bladeling is [specialized](/Rules.aspx?ID=2120), the persistent bleed damage increases to 1d8.

**Advanced Maneuver** Possessing Strike

<document level="2" id="action-3248" />

</column>
name: Gray Bladeling

<title level="1" right="Animal Companion 1" pfs="Standard">[Severed Head](/AnimalCompanions.aspx?ID=121)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Minion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=653" />
<trait label="Undead" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=160" />
</traits>

**Source** [Shining Kingdoms](/Sources.aspx?ID=265) pg. 123

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Characters from Galt have access to this option._

Your companion is an [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722) severed head. The Red Revolution left countless mass graves across Galt, filled with the beheaded corpses of those executed by the [final blades](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1094). Most of the victims’ heads were unceremoniously dumped alongside their bodies, but many were snatched by unscrupulous necromancers who found them to be a convenient (and unfortunately plentiful) raw material for the creation of simple undead minions.

<column gap="tiny">

 

**Size** Small

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws ([finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602)),
**Damage** 1d6 piercing

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +2

**Dex** +1

**Con** +3

**Int** -5

**Wis** +0

**Cha** +0
</row>

**Hit Points** 4

**Skill** none (mindless)

**Senses** [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59)

**Speed** 15 feet, fly 25 feet

**Special** A severed head has the [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722) trait instead of the [animal](/Traits.aspx?ID=531) trait, granting it [void healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=83) and immunity to [death effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=2331), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), and [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669). It’s also [mindless](/Rules.aspx?ID=1690).

**Support Benefit** The head latches on to your enemy with its jaws when you create an opening, distracting them and hampering their movement. The next creature within range of your severed head that you successfully [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) before the start of your next turn takes a –5-foot status penalty to its Speeds and must succeed at a DC 5 [flat check](/Rules.aspx?ID=333) to use reactions triggered by your actions until the start of your next turn. On a failure, the reaction is lost.

**Advanced Maneuver** Gnash

<document level="2" id="action-3249" />

</column>
name: Severed Head

<title level="1" right="Animal Companion 1" pfs="Standard">[Riding Dragonet](/AnimalCompanions.aspx?ID=122)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Dragon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=50" />
<trait label="Minion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=109" />
</traits>

**Source** [Draconic Codex
](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 221

Your companion is a flightless dragonet whose larger-than-average size keeps it grounded. Though typically cagey around other creatures, they have inexplicably attached themself to you, becoming a loving companion. A riding dragonet has the [dragon](/Traits.aspx?ID=581) trait instead of the [animal](/Traits.aspx?ID=531) trait, but it otherwise functions normally as an animal companion.

<column gap="tiny">

 

**Size** Small

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claw ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)),
**Damage** 1d8 bludgeoning

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
tail ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526)),
**Damage** 1d6 slashing

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +1

**Dex** +3

**Con** +2

**Int** -4

**Wis** +1

**Cha** +3
</row>

**Hit Points** 8

**Skill** [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39)

**Senses** [darkvision](/Familiars.aspx?ID=142)

**Speed** 30 feet

**Special** mount

**Support Benefit** Your dragonet’s wings flutter rapidly, pulsating the air around you both. Until the start of your next turn, each time you Strike while riding the dragonet and hit a creature in the dragonet’s reach, the creature takes 1d4 bludgeoning damage from the force of the dragonet’s wings. If your dragonet is [nimble](/Companions.aspx?Type=Advancement&ID=7) or [savage](/Companions.aspx?Type=Advancement&ID=8), the bludgeoning damage increases to 2d4.

**Advanced Maneuver** Enamoring Exhalation

<document level="2" id="action-3480" />

</column>
name: Riding Dragonet

text: Riding Dragonet Source [Draconic Codex ](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 221 Your companion is a flightless dragonet whose larger-than-average size keeps it grounded. Though typically cagey around other creatures, they have inexplicably attached themself to you, becoming a loving companion. A riding dragonet has the dragon trait instead of the animal trait, but it otherwise functions normally as an animal companion. Size Small Melee Single Action claw (Finesse, Magical), Damage 1d8 bludgeoning Melee Single Action tail (Finesse, Magical, Agile), Damage 1d6 slashing Str +1 Dex +3 Con +2 Int -4 Wis +1 Cha +3 Hit Points 8 Skill Diplomacy Senses darkvision Speed 30 feet Special mount Support Benefit Your dragonet’s wings flutter rapidly, pulsating the air around you both. Until the start of your next turn, each time you Strike while riding the dragonet and hit a creature in the dragonet’s reach, the creature takes 1d4 bludgeoning damage from the force of the dragonet’s wings. If your dragonet is nimble or savage, the bludgeoning damage increases to 2d4. Advanced Maneuver Enamoring Exhalation

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Animal Companion 1" pfs="Limited">[Isgeri Boarhound](/AnimalCompanions.aspx?ID=123)</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Hellbreakers</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
</traits>

**Source** [Pathfinder #222: Hellbreakers](/Sources.aspx?ID=307) pg. 215

Through training and selective breeding, the gnomes of Umok found ways to capitalize on the native Isgeri boarhounds' exceptional scent abilities and sturdy frame to patrol against extraplanar predators and other enemies regularly found in the region.

<column gap="tiny">

 

**Size** Small

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602)),
**Damage** 1d8 piercing

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +2

**Dex** +3

**Con** +2

**Int** -4

**Wis** +1

**Cha** +0
</row>

**Hit Points** 6

**Skill** [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49)

**Senses** foe scent (precise, 40 feet), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=2407)) 40 feet

**Speed** 40 feet

**Special** As part of daily preparations, the Isgeri boarhound can be offered a sample of an enemy with the fiend or undead trait, such as a bone, piece of flesh, or similarly scented sample that has been acquired within the last 24 hours. For the next 24 hours, either fiend or undead becomes the boarhound's designated foe. The Isgeri boarhound's foe scent enables it to smell the presence of a creature designated as its foe as a precise scent at a range of 40 feet.

**Support Benefit** Your boarhound is a faithful hunting companion. You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to your next attack roll to Strike a foe within your boarhound's reach. This bonus lasts until the first time you use it or until the beginning of your next turn, whichever comes first. If you damage a creature with the trait corresponding to your boarhound's designated foe with that Strike, the creature takes an additional 1d6 piercing damage from the boarhound. If your boarhound is nimble or savage, the damage increases to 2d6.

**Advanced Maneuver** Lock Jaw

</column>

<document level="2" id="action-3634" />
name: Isgeri Boarhound

Your companion is an alchemical ooze that extends pseudopods to move around.

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Animal Companion 14" pfs="Limited">[Thruneosaurus Rex](/AnimalCompanions.aspx?ID=126)</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Hell's Destiny</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Beast" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=547" />
<trait label="Devil" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=575" />
<trait label="Minion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=653" />
</traits>

**Source** [Pathfinder #223: Hell's Destiny](/Sources.aspx?ID=336) pg. 229

Developed by Chelaxian diabolists, thruneosauruses are powerful therapods infused with Hell’s fury. Few trainers can wrangle such a willful beast, much less check its diabolical urges.

<column gap="tiny">

 

**Size** Large

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws,
**Damage** 1d8 piercing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
foot,
**Damage** 1d8 bludgeoning

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +3

**Dex** +1

**Con** +2

**Int** -3

**Wis** +0

**Cha** +2
</row>

**Hit Points** 8

**Skill** [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40)

**Senses** [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=2407)) 30 feet

**Speed** 35 feet

**Special** **Recessive Fiend** A [sanctified](/Traits.aspx?ID=519) handler can suppress or fully awaken a thruneosaurus’s fiendish influence. Unless you’re [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517), the dinosaur gains the [devil](/Traits.aspx?ID=575) trait. If you’re [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521), the dinosaur, its Strikes, and its advanced maneuver gain the unholy trait.

**Support Benefit** Your thruneosaurus’s horns radiate heat, igniting distracted foes. Until the start of your next turn, your [Strikes](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167) that damage a creature in your thruneosaurus’s reach also deal 1d6 [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86). If your thruneosaurus is [nimble](/Companions.aspx?Type=Advancement&ID=7) or [savage](/Companions.aspx?Type=Advancement&ID=8), the persistent damage increases to 2d6.

**Advanced Maneuver** Hellfire Gaze

</column>

<document level="2" id="action-4229" />
name: Thruneosaurus Rex

Size Medium Melee Single Action jaws, Damage 1d8 piercing Str +3 Dex +2 Con +2 Int -4 Wis +1 Cha +0 Hit Points 8 Skill Survival Senses low-light vision, scent (imprecise) 30 feet Speed 30 feet Special mount Support Benefit The monitor lizard distracts an adjacent opponent with snapping jaws and rapid movements. Until the start of your next turn, if your Strike damages a foe, any time the foe attempts an action with the manipulate trait until the end of your next turn, it must attempt a DC 5 flat check. On a failure, the action is disrupted. Advanced Maneuver Lurching Rush

<title level="1" right="Animal Companion 1" pfs="Standard">[Silt Frog](/AnimalCompanions.aspx?ID=130)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Animal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=9" />
<trait label="Minion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=109" />
</traits>

**Source** [High Seas](/Sources.aspx?ID=351) pg. 109

Your companion is a silt frog, an oversized frog with a natural ability to detect magic, its main food source.

<column gap="tiny">

 

**Size** Small

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws,
**Damage** 1d6 piercing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
tongue ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)),
**Damage** 1d4 bludgeoning

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +3

**Dex** +2

**Con** +2

**Int** -4

**Wis** +1

**Cha** +0
</row>

**Hit Points** 6

**Skill** [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48)

**Senses** [low-light vision](/Rules.aspx?ID=2411), magic sense ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=2407)) 30 feet (the silt frog can sense the presence of magical auras within 30 feet as an imprecise sense)

**Speed** 25 feet

 

**Support Benefit** Your silt frog unleashes an eardrum-rattling croak when you create an opening. Until the start of your next turn, if you hit and deal damage to a creature within your silt frog’s reach, the creature is [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=66) until the start of your next turn.

**Advanced Maneuver** Ingest Magic

</column>

<document level="2" id="action-4234" />

<title level="1" right="Animal Companion 1" pfs="Limited">[Cave Pterosaur](/AnimalCompanions.aspx?ID=14)</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Extinction Curse</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Animal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=531" />
<trait label="Minion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=653" />
</traits>

**Source** [Pathfinder #154: Siege of the Dinosaurs](/Sources.aspx?ID=31) pg. 74

Your companion is a flying prehistoric creature, such as a dimorphodon or pterodactyl, that has adapted to life in the cavern vaults beneath the ground.

<column gap="tiny">

 

**Size** Small

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
beak ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)),
**Damage** 1d6 piercing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
talon ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)),
**Damage** 1d4 slashing

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +2

**Dex** +3

**Con** +1

**Int** -4

**Wis** +2

**Cha** +0
</row>

**Hit Points** 4

**Skill** [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17)

**Senses** [darkvision](/Rules.aspx?ID=415)

**Speed** 10 feet, fly 60 feet

 

**Support Benefit** Your pterosaur expands its wings and makes distracting flapping motions. Any creature that would gain lesser cover from the pterosaur gains standard cover instead.

**Advanced Maneuver** Pterosaur Swoop

</column>

<title level="1" right="Animal Companion 1" pfs="Standard">[Arboreal Sapling](/AnimalCompanions.aspx?ID=17)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Minion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=653" />
<trait label="Plant" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=125" />
</traits>

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 144

Your companion is a walking tree, a cousin of the great arboreal wardens and regents. An arboreal sapling has the [plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=125) trait instead of the [animal](/Traits.aspx?ID=9) trait, but it otherwise functions normally as an animal companion. They are typically only selected by [animal order](/DruidicOrders.aspx?ID=1) druids who are also members of the [leaf order](/DruidicOrders.aspx?ID=2).

<column gap="tiny">

**Access** You are a member of the [leaf order](/DruidicOrders.aspx?ID=2).

**Size** Small

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
branch,
**Damage** 1d8 bludgeoning

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +3

**Dex** +1

**Con** +2

**Int** -4

**Wis** +2

**Cha** +0
</row>

**Hit Points** 8

**Skill** [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15)

**Senses** [low-light vision](/Rules.aspx?ID=416)

**Speed** 25 feet

 

**Support Benefit** Your arboreal sapling plants roots around your foe, impeding its movement. Until the start of your next turn, if you hit and deal damage to a creature in your arboreal sapling's reach, the first square the creature moves into after taking that damage is difficult terrain.

**Advanced Maneuver** Throw Rock

</column>

<document level="2" id="action-556" />
name: Arboreal Sapling

<trait label="Minion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=653" />
</traits>

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 144

Your companion is a particularly large bat, such as a [giant bat](/Monsters.aspx?ID=46).

<column gap="tiny">

 

**Size** Small

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)),

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Animal Companion 1" pfs="Standard">[Crocodile](/AnimalCompanions.aspx?ID=20)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Animal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=531" />
<trait label="Minion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=653" />
</traits>

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 145

Your companion is a crocodile or a similar crocodilian reptile, such as an alligator or caiman.

<column gap="tiny">

 

**Size** Small

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws,
**Damage** 1d8 piercing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
tail ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)),
**Damage** 1d6 bludgeoning

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +3

**Dex** +2

**Con** +2

**Int** -4

**Wis** +1

**Cha** +0
</row>

**Hit Points** 6

**Skill** [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15)

**Senses** [low-light vision](/Rules.aspx?ID=416)

**Speed** 20 feet, swim 25 feet

**Special** The crocodile can hold its breath for about 2 hours.

**Support Benefit** Your crocodile clamps its jaws on your foe, refusing to let go. Until the start of your next turn, if your Strike damages a creature in your crocodile's reach, your crocodile can latch onto the creature. While thus attached, the crocodile can move along with the target whenever the target moves until the end of your next turn. Your crocodile can latch onto only one creature in this way, and it must release the creature to make a jaws Strike. If the target is smaller than the crocodile, it takes a –10-foot circumstance penalty to its Speeds and can't [Fly](/Actions.aspx?ID=94) while the crocodile is latched on.

**Advanced Maneuver** Death Roll

</column>

<document level="2" id="action-559" />

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Dragon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=50" />
<trait label="Minion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=653" />
</traits>

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 145

Your companion is a swift, flightless drake with limited intelligence, less vicious than most drakes but fiercely loyal to you. A riding drake has the [dragon](/Traits.aspx?ID=50) trait instead of the [animal](/Traits.aspx?ID=9) trait, but it otherwise functions normally as an animal companion.

<column gap="tiny">

 

**Size** Large

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws,
**Damage** 1d8 piercing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
tail,
**Damage** 1d6 bludgeoning

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +2

**Dex** +1

**Con** +2

**Int** -4

**Wis** +1

**Cha** +2
</row>

**Hit Points** 8

**Skill** [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7)

**Senses** [darkvision](/Rules.aspx?ID=415)

**Speed** 45 feet

**Special** [mount](/Rules.aspx?ID=150)

**Support Benefit** Your drake snorts fire in your foes' faces. Until the start of your next turn, each time you Strike while riding the drake and hit a creature in the drake's reach, the creature takes 1d4 fire damage from the drake. If your drake is nimble or savage, the fire damage increases to 2d4. 

**Advanced Maneuver** Breath Weapon

</column>

text: Riding Drake Source Advanced Player's Guide pg. 145 Your companion is a swift, flightless drake with limited intelligence, less vicious than most drakes but fiercely loyal to you. A riding drake has the dragon trait instead of the animal trait, but it otherwise functions normally as an animal companion. Size Large Melee Single Action jaws, Damage 1d8 piercing Melee Single Action tail, Damage 1d6 bludgeoning Str +2 Dex +1 Con +2 Int -4 Wis +1 Cha +2 Hit Points 8 Skill Intimidation Senses darkvision Speed 45 feet Special mount Support Benefit Your drake snorts fire in your foes' faces. Until the start of your next turn, each time you Strike while riding the drake and hit a creature in the drake's reach, the creature takes 1d4 fire damage from the drake. If your drake is nimble or savage, the fire damage increases to 2d4. Advanced Maneuver Breath Weapon

<title level="1" right="Animal Companion 1" pfs="Standard">[Scorpion](/AnimalCompanions.aspx?ID=22)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Animal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=531" />
<trait label="Minion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=653" />
</traits>

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 145

Your companion is one of the various species of giant scorpion.

<column gap="tiny">

 

**Size** Small

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
stinger,
**Damage** 1d6 piercing plus poison

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
pincer ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)),
**Damage** 1d6 slashing

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +3

**Dex** +3

**Con** +1

**Int** -4

**Wis** +1

**Cha** +0
</row>

**Hit Points** 6

**Skill** [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15)

**Senses** [darkvision](/Rules.aspx?ID=415)

**Speed** 30 feet

**Special** The scorpion's stinger attack deals an additional 1d4 poison damage or 2d4 poison damage if the scorpion is a specialized companion.

**Support Benefit** Your scorpion drips poison from its stinger when you create an opening. Until the start of your next turn, your Strikes that damage a creature in your scorpion's reach also deal 1d6 [persistent poison damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29). If your scorpion is nimble or savage, the persistent poison damage increases to 2d6.

**Advanced Maneuver** Grab and Sting

</column>

<title level="1" right="Animal Companion 1" pfs="Standard">[Shark](/AnimalCompanions.aspx?ID=23)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Animal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=531" />
<trait label="Aquatic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=168" />
<trait label="Minion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=653" />
</traits>

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 145

Your companion is a shark of any kind, such as a hammerhead, great white, or jigsaw shark.

<column gap="tiny">

 

**Size** Small

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws,
**Damage** 1d8 piercing

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +3

**Dex** +2

**Con** +2

**Int** -4

**Wis** +1

**Cha** +0
</row>

**Hit Points** 6

**Skill** [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15)

**Senses** blood scent, [scent](/Rules.aspx?ID=417) (imprecise, 60 feet)

**Speed** swim 40 feet

**Special** The shark has the [aquatic](/Traits.aspx?ID=168) trait. Its blood scent enables it to smell blood in the water from up to 1 mile away.

**Support Benefit** When your shark senses blood, it tears into your enemies. Until the start of your next turn, each time you hit a creature in the shark's reach with a Strike and deal slashing or piercing damage, the creature takes 1d8 slashing damage from the shark. If your shark is nimble or savage, the slashing damage increases to 2d8.

**Advanced Maneuver** Shred

</column>

<trait label="Animal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=531" />
<trait label="Minion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=653" />
</traits>

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 228

Your companion is a canine creature related to the [Shadow Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=11) denizens known as shadow mastiffs.

<column gap="tiny">

**Access** You're a [shadowcaster](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=101)

**Size** Small

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)),
**Damage** 1d8 piercing

<row gap="medium">

**Dex** +3

**Con** +1

**Int** -4

**Wis** +1

**Cha** +1
</row>

**Hit Points** 4

**Skill** [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7)

**Senses** [darkvision](/Rules.aspx?ID=415), [scent](/Rules.aspx?ID=417) (imprecise) 30 feet

**Speed** 30 feet

 

**Support Benefit** Your shadow hound shrouds your foes in shadow when you leave an opening. Until the start of your next turn, when you damage a creature with a Strike and that creature is within reach of your shadow hound, both you and your shadow hound become [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) to the creature until the end of your next turn.

**Advanced Maneuver** Bay

<trait label="Minion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=653" />
</traits>

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 54

Your animal companion is a large, flightless stag beetle.

<column gap="tiny">

 

**Size** Medium or Large

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
mandible,
**Damage** 1d8 piercing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
foot ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)),
**Damage** 1d6 bludgeoning

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +3

**Dex** +1

**Con** +3

**Int** -4

**Wis** +1

**Cha** +0
</row>

**Hit Points** 8

**Skill** [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16)

**Senses** [low-light vision](/Rules.aspx?ID=416)

**Speed** 35 feet

**Special** [mount](/Rules.aspx?ID=150)

**Support Benefit** Your beetle stomps around and smashes into foes, knocking them off balance or pushing them aside when you create an opening. Until the start of your next turn, while riding on your beetle, your Strikes that deal damage either make the target [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) until the end of your next turn or move the target 5 feet away from the beetle (this is [forced movement](/Rules.aspx?ID=451)). The target chooses which effect occurs.

**Advanced Maneuver** Hustle

<trait label="Minion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=653" />
</traits>

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 54

Your animal companion is a giant fruit-eating moth with a strong proboscis that can pierce skin.

<column gap="tiny">

 

**Size** Small

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
proboscis ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)),
**Damage** 1d6 piercing

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +2

**Dex** +3

**Con** +1

**Int** -4

**Wis** +2

**Cha** +0
</row>

**Hit Points** 6

**Skill** [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15)

**Senses** [darkvision](/Rules.aspx?ID=415), [scent](/Rules.aspx?ID=417) (imprecise, 30 feet)

**Speed** 15 feet, fly 30 feet

**Special** Your moth is invisible to echolocation.

**Support Benefit** Your moth releases a shower of dust to reveal your foes. Until the start of your next turn, if you damage a [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) or [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=22) creature in your moth's reach with a Strike, that creature requires only a DC 3 flat check to target it if it's concealed, or DC 9 if it's hidden. **Advanced Maneuver** Ultrasonic Scream

Size Small Melee Single Action jaws (Finesse), Damage 1d6 piercing Melee Single Action talon (Agile, Finesse), Damage 1d4 slashing Str +2 Dex +3 Con +1 Int -4 Wis +2 Cha +0 Hit Points 4 Skill Stealth Senses low-light vision Speed 10 feet, fly 60 feet Support Benefit The bird pecks at your foes’ eyes when you create an opening. Until the start of your next turn, your Strikes that damage a creature that your bird threatens also deal 1d4 persistent bleed damage, and the target is dazzled until it removes the bleed damage. If your bird is nimble or savage, the persistent bleed damage increases to 2d4. Advanced Maneuver Flyby Attack

<traits>
<trait label="Animal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=531" />
<trait label="Minion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=653" />
</traits>

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 72

Your companion is a strange creature, akin to a chair with bestial legs.

<column gap="tiny">

 

**Size** Medium or Large

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
hoof ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)),
**Damage** 1d6 bludgeoning

<row gap="medium">

**Dex** +2

**Con** +2

**Int** -4

**Wis** +1

**Cha** +0
</row>

**Hit Points** 8

**Skill** [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15)

**Senses** [low-light vision](/Rules.aspx?ID=416), [scent](/Rules.aspx?ID=417) (imprecise, 30 feet)

**Speed** 40 feet

**Special** [mount](/Rules.aspx?ID=150)

**Support Benefit** Your legchair moves its limbs in the way of enemy attacks. You gain lesser cover from your legchair against all attacks, not just ones where the legchair would be in the way.

**Advanced Maneuver** Careful Withdraw

</column>

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Animal Companion 1" pfs="Standard">[Ulgrem-Lurann](/AnimalCompanions.aspx?ID=33)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Amphibious" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=207" />
<trait label="Beast" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=20" />
<trait label="Minion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=653" />
</traits>

**Source** [Monsters of Myth](/Sources.aspx?ID=107) pg. 119

Your companion is one of the few surviving members of a formerly lost species, hatched from the last clutch of Ulgrem-Axaan. As it's so young, its intelligence hasn't developed yet, but some day, it will become a highly intelligent creature. An Ulgrem-Lurann has the [beast](/Traits.aspx?ID=20) trait instead of the [animal](/Traits.aspx?ID=9) trait but otherwise functions normally as an animal companion.

<column gap="tiny">

 

**Size** Medium

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws,
**Damage** 1d8 piercing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
tail,
**Damage** 1d6 bludgeoning

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +3

**Dex** +2

**Con** +2

**Int** -4

**Wis** +0

**Cha** +1
</row>

**Hit Points** 6

**Skill** [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7)

**Senses** [darkvision](/Rules.aspx?ID=415)

**Speed** 20 feet, swim 25 feet

**Special** The Ulgrem-Lurann has the [amphibious](/Traits.aspx?ID=207) trait.

**Support Benefit** The Ulgrem-Lurann grabs at your foe and zaps it. Until the start of your next turn, each time you hit a creature in the Ulgrem-Lurann's reach with a Strike, the creature takes an additional 1d4 piercing damage and 1d4 electricity damage from the Ulgrem-Lurann. If the Ulgrem-Lurann is nimble or savage, the piercing damage and electricity damage each increase to 2d4.

**Advanced Maneuver** Snatch and Zap

</column>

<document level="2" id="action-1110" />
name: Ulgrem-Lurann

</traits>

**Source** [Pathfinder #177: Burning Tundra](/Sources.aspx?ID=116) pg. 73

Your companion is an elephant or mammoth.

<column gap="tiny">

 

**Size** Large

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
tusk,
**Damage** 1d8 piercing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
foot,
**Damage** 1d6 bludgeoning

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +3

**Dex** +1

**Con** +2

**Int** -4

**Wis** +2

**Cha** +0

**Source** [Pathfinder #177: Burning Tundra](/Sources.aspx?ID=116) pg. 73

Your companion is a triceratops or other armored dinosaur. If necessary, change the horn attack and damage type to an appropriate attack for the dinosaur (for example, an ankylosaurus would have a tail attack that deals bludgeoning damage).

<column gap="tiny">

 

**Size** Large

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
horn,
**Damage** 1d8 piercing

**Melee**

**Int** -4

**Senses** [low-light vision](/Rules.aspx?ID=416), [scent](/Rules.aspx?ID=417) (imprecise, 30 feet)

**Speed** 25 feet

**Special** [mount](/Rules.aspx?ID=150)

**Support Benefit** Your triceratops gores [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31) targets in your wake. Until the start of your next turn, your Strikes that damage a prone creature in your triceratops's reach also deal 1d8 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29). If your triceratops is nimble or savage, the persistent bleed damage increases to 2d8.

**Advanced Maneuver** Lumbering Knockdown

</column>

<title level="1" right="Animal Companion 1" pfs="Standard">[Ghost](/AnimalCompanions.aspx?ID=38)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Incorporeal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=632" />
<trait label="Minion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=653" />
<trait label="Undead" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=722" />
</traits>

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 38

Your companion is a [ghost](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=51), bound to an item in your possession. Due to this bond, your ghost companion can move beyond the area of a single dwelling, but it's easier to disrupt than most incorporeal creatures. A ghost companion can be formed from a [humanoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=91), [animal](/Traits.aspx?ID=9), or other creature. The ghost isn't sapient and is unable to speak with the living.

<column gap="tiny">

 

**Size** Small or Medium

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
ghostly touch ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)),
**Damage** 1d8 negative (doesn't apply the ghost's Strength modifier)

<row gap="medium">
**Str** -5

**Dex** +3

**Con** +0

**Int** -4

**Wis** +1

**Cha** +2
</row>

**Hit Points** 4

**Skill** [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15)

**Senses** [darkvision](/Rules.aspx?ID=415)

**Speed** fly 20 feet

**Special** [anchored incorporeality](/Rules.aspx?ID=1688)

**Support Benefit** Your ghost spooks and scares your foes as you attack them. Until the start of your next turn, if you hit and deal damage to a creature in your ghost's reach, the creature becomes [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19).

**Advanced Maneuver** Telekinetic Assault

</column>

<title level="1" right="Animal Companion 1" pfs="Standard">[Vampiric Animal](/AnimalCompanions.aspx?ID=41)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Minion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=653" />
<trait label="Undead" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=722" />
</traits>

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 39

Your companion is a wolf, weasel, fox, or other predator with fangs and the blood thirst of a vampire. They often serve vampires who willingly shared their blood to bind the animal to their vampiric whims.

<column gap="tiny">

 

**Size** Small or Medium

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)),
**Damage** 1d8 piercing

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +2

**Dex** +2

**Con** +1

**Int** -4

**Wis** +1

**Cha** +0
</row>

**Hit Points** 4

**Skill** [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16)

**Senses** [low-light vision](/Rules.aspx?ID=416), [scent](/Rules.aspx?ID=417) (imprecise, 30 feet)

**Speed** 30 feet

**Special** Vampiric animal companions are particularly attuned to the scent of blood. If a creature has [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29), your vampiric animal companion can smell the creature if it's within 120 feet rather than 30 feet.

**Support Benefit** When your vampiric animal senses blood it tears into your enemies. Until the start of your next turn, your Strikes that damage a creature in your vampiric animal's reach also deal 1d6 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29). If your vampiric animal is nimble or savage, the persistent bleed damage increases to 2d6.

**Advanced Maneuver** Blood Feast

</column>

<title level="1" right="Animal Companion 1" pfs="Standard">[Skeletal Mount](/AnimalCompanions.aspx?ID=43)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Mindless" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=652" />
<trait label="Minion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=653" />
<trait label="Undead" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=722" />
</traits>

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 38

Your companion is a skeletal horse, drake, elk, or other animal suitable for riding. Skeletal mounts can be collapsed, gathered into piles of bone, and packed away for storage.

<column gap="tiny">

 

**Size** Large

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
hoof ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)),
**Damage** 1d6 bludgeoning

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +2

**Dex** +2

**Con** +2

**Int** -5

**Wis** +0

**Cha** +0
</row>

**Hit Points** 4

**Skill** none (mindless)

**Senses** [darkvision](/Rules.aspx?ID=415)

**Speed** 35 feet

**Special** [mindless](/Rules.aspx?ID=1690), [mount](/Rules.aspx?ID=150)

**Support Benefit** Your skeletal mount strikes fear when it charges. Until the start of your next turn, if you are riding your skeletal mount and move at least 10 feet on the action before your attack, any creature damaged by the attack becomes [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19), or frightened 2 if the attack was a critical hit. This is an emotion, fear, and mental effect.

**Advanced Maneuver** Gallop

</column>

<trait label="Undead" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=722" />
</traits>

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 38

Your companion is a humanoid skeleton, mindless but capable of assisting in rudimentary chores. Like the [skeletal mount](/Companions.aspx?ID=43), it can be collapsed for easy storage.

<column gap="tiny">

 

**Size** Small or Medium

<actions string="Single Action" />

**Int** -5

**Wis** +0

**Cha** +1
</row>

**Hit Points** 4

**Skill** none (mindless)

**Senses** [darkvision](/Rules.aspx?ID=415)

**Speed** 20 feet

**Special** [mindless](/Rules.aspx?ID=1690)

**Support Benefit** Your skeletal servant jabs bones into your enemies to distract them while you attack. Until the start of your next turn, when a Strike you make hits and deals damage to a creature within your skeletal servant's reach, that target becomes [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) until the end of your next turn.

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Mindless" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=652" />
<trait label="Minion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=653" />
<trait label="Undead" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=722" />
</traits>

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 39

Your companion is a mindless [zombie](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=103) created from a soulless dead body, be it [animal](/Traits.aspx?ID=9) or [humanoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=91). The stats below list a fist Strike, but this can be replaced by a claw or similar option better suited to the creature's form.

<column gap="tiny">

 

**Size** Small or Medium

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist,
**Damage** 1d8 piercing

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +2

**Dex** +0

**Con** +4

**Int** -5

**Wis** +0

**Cha** +0
</row>

**Hit Points** 10

**Skill** none (mindless)

**Senses** [darkvision](/Rules.aspx?ID=415)

**Speed** 20 feet

**Special** [mindless](/Rules.aspx?ID=1690)

**Support Benefit** The zombie emits an aura of rot, causing wounds to fester. Until the start of your next turn, each time you hit a creature that's within 10 feet of the zombie and you deal damage, the creature takes an additional 1d6 damage as the wound festers. If your zombie is nimble or savage, the damage increases to 2d6. This is a [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46) and [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117) effect.

**Advanced Maneuver** Take a Taste

</column>

Your companion is the skeleton of a bird of prey like a falcon or an owl.

<column gap="tiny">

**Melee**

**Senses** [darkvision](/Rules.aspx?ID=415)

<traits>

**Source** [Pathfinder #181: Zombie Feast](/Sources.aspx?ID=139) pg. 78

Your companion is the zombified corpse of a large scavenging bird, such as a vulture or a raven, that subsists upon meat.

<column gap="tiny">

 

**Size** Small

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws,
**Damage** 1d8 piercing

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +2

**Dex** +1

**Con** +3

**Int** -5

**Wis** +0

**Cha** +0
</row>

**Hit Points** 6

**Skill** none (mindless)

**Senses** [darkvision](/Rules.aspx?ID=415)

**Speed** 10 feet, fly 30 feet

**Special** [mindless](/Traits.aspx?ID=108)

**Support Benefit** The zombie emits an aura of rot, causing wounds to fester. Until the start of your next turn, each time you hit and deal damage to a creature that's within 10 feet of the zombie carrion bird, the creature takes an additional 1d6 damage as the wound festers. If your carrion bird is nimble or savage, the damage increases to 2d6. This is a [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46) and [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117) effect.

**Advanced Maneuver** Pick at the Meat

</column>

<document level="2" id="action-1270" />

<title level="1" right="Animal Companion 1" pfs="Limited">[Zombie Mount](/AnimalCompanions.aspx?ID=52)</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Blood Lords</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Mindless" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=652" />
<trait label="Minion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=653" />
<trait label="Undead" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=722" />
</traits>

**Source** [Pathfinder #181: Zombie Feast](/Sources.aspx?ID=139) pg. 78

Your companion is the rotting, reanimated corpse of a horse, camel, or similar riding animal.

<column gap="tiny">

 

**Size** Large

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
hoof,
**Damage** 1d8 bludgeoning

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +2

**Dex** +1

**Con** +3

**Int** -5

**Wis** +0

**Cha** +0
</row>

**Hit Points** 8

**Skill** none (mindless)

**Senses** [darkvision](/Rules.aspx?ID=415)

**Speed** 30 feet

**Special** [mindless](/Traits.aspx?ID=108), [mount](/Rules.aspx?ID=150)

**Support Benefit** Your zombie mount chews on your enemies. Until the start of your next turn, each time you Strike while riding the zombie mount and hit a creature in the zombie mount's reach, the creature takes 1d4 bludgeoning damage from the zombie mount. If your zombie mount is nimble or savage, the bludgeoning damage increases to 2d4. This benefit is based on a zombie horse or camel, but might deal piercing damage if your mount has sharp teeth.

**Advanced Maneuver** Disgusting Gallop

</column>

<title level="1" right="Animal Companion 1" pfs="Standard">[Water Wraith](/AnimalCompanions.aspx?ID=54)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Animal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=531" />
<trait label="Minion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=653" />
</traits>

**Source** [Impossible Lands](/Sources.aspx?ID=146) pg. 107

Your companion is a water wraith, an amphibious and expressive lizard capable of changing the color of its scales as it pleases. While your companion hasn't yet matured into its full strength and intelligence, it still serves you with admirable tenacity and loyalty, not to mention its lamprey-like mouth of lacerating teeth and eight clawed, slashing feet.

<column gap="tiny">

**Access** Characters from Alkenstar have access to this companion

**Size** Small

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws,
**Damage** 1d8 piercing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claw ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)),
**Damage** 1d6 slashing

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +3

**Dex** +2

**Con** +2

**Int** -4

**Wis** +1

**Cha** +0
</row>

**Hit Points** 6

**Skill** [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16)

**Senses** Normal

**Speed** 20 feet, swim 25 feet

**Special** The water wraith can hold its breath for about 2 hours.

**Support Benefit** Your water wraith hisses and changes colors swiftly in an iridescent clash, overwhelming your foes in an avalanche of shades and hues. Until the start of your next turn, each time you hit a creature within your water wraith's reach, the creature is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7) for 1 round.

**Advanced Maneuver** Gulp Blood

</column>

<document level="2" id="action-1453" />

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 106

Your companion is an alchemical ooze that extends pseudopods to move around.

<column gap="tiny">

 

**Size** Medium or Large

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
pseudopod ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)),

**Wis** +0

**Cha** +0
</row>

**Senses** **Motion Sense** Your oozeform chair can sense nearby creatures through vibration in the air. This is an [imprecise sense](/Rules.aspx?ID=412) with a range of 30 feet.

**Speed** 20 feet

**Special** [mount](/Rules.aspx?ID=150)

**Support Benefit** A pseudopod extends out from your chair, retrieving an unattended object within 15 feet. If you don't have a hand free to receive the object, the chair drops it in your space.

**Advanced Maneuver** Extend Pseudopod

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Druids of the specifically listed orders may also take the other elementals (e.g. a wave order druid may take a water elemental companion)._

Your companion is an air elemental, such as a [zephyr hawk](/Monsters.aspx?ID=177).

<column gap="tiny">

**Access** You are a member of the [storm order](/DruidicOrders.aspx?ID=3).

**Size** Small

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
gust ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)),
**Damage** 1d6 bludgeoning

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +1

**Dex** +2

**Con** +1

**Int** -4

**Wis** +1

**Cha** +1
</row>

**Hit Points** 4

**Skill** [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15)

**Senses** [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12)

**Speed** fly 50 feet

 

**Support Benefit** Your air elemental swirls around you with concealing gusts, making it harder for your foes to hit you and possibly giving you a moment to hide. Until the start of your next turn, if you hit with a Strike and deal damage to a creature in your air elemental's reach, you are [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) to that creature until the start of your next turn.

**Advanced Maneuver** Circling Flyby

</traits>

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 40

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Druids of the specifically listed orders may also take the other elementals (e.g. a wave order druid may take a water elemental companion)._

Your companion is a fire elemental, such as a [cinder rat](/Monsters.aspx?ID=187) or [lava otter](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2633).

<column gap="tiny">

**Access** You are a member of the [flame order](/DruidicOrders.aspx?ID=5).

**Size** Small

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
tendril ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)),

**Dex** +2

**Con** +1

**Int** -4

**Wis** +1

**Cha** +0
</row>

**Advanced Maneuver** Flamethrower
